arts.ttl: Gittens to pick Arsenal and reject Chelsea, Man Utd: Dortmund star’s big transfer decision
arts.sum: Borussia Dortmund’s Jamie Gittens is a wanted man, with the young Englishman coveted by a string of Premier League giants including Arsenal, Chelsea and Manchester United. Jamie Gittens: Why Arsenal are Dortmund star's best option

arts.ttl: Revealed: Liverpool & Newcastle eyeing €45m star who nearly joined Arsenal
arts.sum: Liverpool and Newcastle United are now among the suitors for the potential transfer of Bayern Munich winger Kingsley Coman ahead of the summer. The France international is set to be available for around €45m after suffering a bit of a dip in form for Bayern, and he’d be open to a move to the Premier […] The post Revealed: Liverpool & Newcastle eyeing €45m star who nearly joined Arsenal appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Arsenal rivals have 'little chance' of signing Benjamin Sesko amid Chelsea and Man Utd update
arts.sum: Besides Arsenal, United and Chelsea are said to have expressed 'concrete interest.' Sesko's contract is reported to have a formally fixed release clause for the summer which could rise to €80million (£66m) by the end of the season. The north Londoners have been long-term admirers of Sesko and tried to sign him last year before the striker decided to extend his stay at RB Leipzig.

arts.ttl: Thierry Henry delivers Arsenal Champions League verdict with three big reasons given
arts.sum: Thierry Henry believes Arsenal's 7-1 Champions League win over PSV Eindhoven will instil confidence in the team to compete for European glory. In the middle of having to deal with an abrupt end to the Premier League title race, Arsenal are guaranteed to progress to the quarter-finals of the competition and could get a major boost before facing either Real Madrid or Atletico Madrid.

arts.ttl: PSV boss points at two moments which could have changed game before 7-1 defeat to Arsenal
arts.sum: Arsenal smashed PSV Eindhoven 7-1 in the first leg of their Champions League last 16 clash, but the Dutch side's manager insisted things could have turned out differently

arts.ttl: Viktor Gyokeres transfer news: Arsenal move 'ruled out' by Mikel Arteta
arts.sum: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta has ruled out a possible summer move for Sporting CP forward Viktor Gyokeres, according to a sensational report in Spain. Barcelona, on the other hand, remain interested in signing Gyokeres, although Hansi Flick’s side are reportedly aware that a deal is likely to be complicated due to Sporting’s asking price of €70m and their current financial woes.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta celebrates new Arsenal record with fresh challenge to Gunners stars
arts.sum: Arsenal enjoyed a record-breaking night in Eindhoven as Mikel Arteta's men put their goalscoring struggles behind them to utterly destroy PSV with a 7-1 rout

arts.ttl: Jamie Carragher named three Arsenal 'joke' signings and they all ended the same way
arts.sum: Arsenal transfers have not always ended positively despite the club finding a greater success rate in recent times and Jamie Carragher was heavily critical of the club in 2020

arts.ttl: Arsenal player ratings vs PSV as Odegaard reborn, Nwaneri and Timber excellent
arts.sum: Then Ethan Nwaneri doubled the Gunners’ lead, pouncing on a cross from Myles Lewis-Skelly who would be replaced by Riccardo Calafiori having escaped what looked a nailed-on second yellow card offence. He scored his second with a powerful strike from outside the box before slipping in Calafiori to score the seventh Arsenal goal of the night.

arts.ttl: What Mikel Arteta will do before Champions League quarter-finals as Arsenal land huge advantage
arts.sum: As the season now looks to be coming down to how far in this competition Arsenal can go, stepping up in the Netherlands was an indicator of champion quality in a young team determined to prove themselves. If Arsenal had continued a mini-slump at PSV then all the pressure would have been on to eek something out of the remaining fixtures until May. As it happens, the players will be on somewhat of a victory lap before the return leg and they will then be cheered on at home before being sent off to one side of Madrid with dreams of the semi-final.
